actions._comment: Three scripts enqueued: (1) main session prompt — long explanation + apply_patch tool call; (2) post-tool-result follow-up text; (3) title-generation small-model call. Title gen fires in parallel with the main call, so script order is by FIFO race winner. With three scripts queued the post-tool follow-up always finds a real script instead of falling back to the default 'Simulation mock response.'

steps.content: Looking at `src/greeting.ts`, the function currently returns `"Hi, …"`.
You asked for a friendlier greeting, so I'll change the prefix from `Hi` to `Hello`.
I'll keep the template literal and the `name` interpolation untouched.
Here's the plan: I'll use the `apply_patch` tool to replace the single return line.

input.patchText:
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: /opencode/src/greeting.ts
@@
-  return `Hi, ${name}`
+  return `Hello, ${name}`
*** End Patch

actions.text: Make the greeting friendlier — use "Hello" instead of "Hi" in src/greeting.ts.
